description_long: This house features a prominent gable roof with a reddish-brown color and simple geometric lines, evoking a traditional European or Tudor-inspired style. The exterior walls are light gray with dark brown timber framing, creating a half-timbered appearance. The structure is elevated on short stilts or posts, giving it a slightly raised profile above the ground. The roof is adorned with a few small, flat tiles or shingles for added detail. There are no visible windows or doors, and the facade is symmetrical with clean, angular lines. The overall design is minimalistic, focusing on the interplay of roof shapes and timber accents.
description: A raised, half-timbered house with a reddish-brown gable roof and light gray walls, featuring a simple, symmetrical design and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central gable roof and symmetrical half-timbered facade, with no visible entryway or windows.
description_view_1: Similar to the front, this view highlights the roof's depth and the angular intersection of the gable with the main roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the broad expanse of the main roof and the absence of architectural features like doors or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side of the front, again showing the gable projection and symmetrical wall pattern.
attribute_entryway_design: No visible entryway; facade is plain with no steps, porch, or decorative elements.
